title: [SimplifyLibCalls] Avoid simplifying pow(x, 2.0) -> x * x with math-errno.
author: rj-jesus
description:
It came up in https://gcc.gnu.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=123826 that GCC was simplifying pow(x, 2.0) -> x * x, even when doing so caused -fmath-errno to be ignored. LLVM currently behaves the same (https://godbolt.org/z/Wbx567vab).

As [discussed on discourse](https://discourse.llvm.org/t/constantfolding-why-are-fp32-calls-folded-in-double-precision/89878), this patch attempts to fix the problem for the free variable case. I'll open a separate PR for the ConstantFolding part of the issue.

comments.author: rj-jesus
comments.body:
> Is there test coverage for a callsite marked with memory(none) / whatever the spelling is for no-errno

Should be in `pow2_strict_noerrno` and `pow2_double_strict_noerrno`.
